server: python3 -m tests.browser.serve_fixture (REAL backend+Shell, single-threaded loopback, real temp Weft)

tests.title: nav is keyboard-operable, controls named, status has text, no dup ids, landmarks present
import >=3 docs, trust-zoned, with durable provenance across refresh/restart/rebuild
create project (durable), start/pause plan, inspect bounded agent, gated proposal defers
hostile content is inert; only the real trusted component can approve
unauth /api/* is 401 and health is public
strict CSP header is present on static and API responses
no console errors or failed same-origin requests across a full nav walk

product_bugs_found_and_fixed: app.css: login gate overlaid the app after login (hidden attribute defeated by .gate/.app display) -> global [hidden]{display:none!important}
shell/serve.py: ThreadingWSGIServer + single-threaded sqlite Weft 500'd on every authenticated read -> single-threaded make_loopback_server; kernel root fix filed in known-issues.md
projects.js: inline style attribute for progress bar violated style-src 'self' CSP -> width set via CSSOM
documented_product_gaps: Scenario A: no cross-source Q&A / clickable citations (knowledge = trust-zoned notes + durable provenance)
Scenario B: no model-generated plan/accept and no runtime agent hierarchy from the UI (bounded agent is a canonical-kernel harness precondition)
Scenario C: no coding-workspace surface in the Shell (worker isolation covered by tests/adversarial/test_worker_isolation.py)
